title: InvenioAI — Enterprise Document Intelligence Platform
summary: Enterprise document intelligence platform enabling semantic search and question answering using advanced hybrid retrieval (Qdrant + BM25), FastAPI, LangChain, LlamaParse, and Docker.
overview: InvenioAI is an enterprise document intelligence platform that parses complex documents and implements semantic search and question answering using advanced Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG).
architectureText: Processes diverse file types via LlamaParse, indexes chunked passages into Qdrant VectorDB, and runs a low-latency hybrid search pipeline combining Qdrant dense vectors with BM25 lexical keyword scores, followed by high-fidelity reranking.
codeBlock:
from qdrant_client import QdrantClient
from langchain.retrievers import ContextualCompressionRetriever

# Initialize hybrid vector store with Qdrant and BM25
client = QdrantClient(url="http://localhost:6333")
retriever = ContextualCompressionRetriever(
    base_compressor=cohere_rerank,
    base_retriever=qdrant_vector_store.as_retriever(search_kwargs={"k": 10})
)
results: Dynamic Parsing: Seamless parsing of charts, tables, and complex document layouts using LlamaParse.
API Services: Wrapped inside a high-throughput FastAPI application containerized with Docker.
Search Accuracy: Drastically reduced hallucination rates and improved context recall across enterprise-scale PDFs and slide decks.

title: Omnius — AI-Powered Media Intelligence Platform
summary: Media intelligence platform utilizing LLM-powered multi-agent flows (Pydantic AI, Llama 3.3, Qwen3) on Microsoft Azure to perform automated news retrieval, framing, bias, and sentiment analysis.
overview: Omnius is an AI-powered media intelligence platform designed to retrieve, analyze, and extract deep framing, narrative bias, and sentiment metrics from global news content.
architectureText: Orchestrates scalable, structured AI agent pipelines built with Pydantic AI, leveraging state-of-the-art models like Llama 3.3 and Qwen3. These agents autonomously coordinate news ingestion, relevance checks, and deep cognitive sentiment evaluations.
codeBlock:
from pydantic_ai import Agent
from pydantic import BaseModel

class NewsAnalysis(BaseModel):
    framing_bias: str
    sentiment_score: float
    key_narratives: list[str]

# Define LLM Agent
analysis_agent = Agent('azure:llama-3.3-70b-instruct', result_type=NewsAnalysis)
results: Automated Agent Flow: Retrieves news and classifies narratives according to political framing theories.
FastAPI & Docker: Implemented as modular microservices, deployed as highly scalable workloads on Microsoft Azure.
Robust Schemas: Enforces strong response schemas and structural validations using Pydantic AI.

title: LUCIAN — Lung Carcinoma Histopathology Imaging & Analysis
summary: Built a lung cancer histopathology classification system using ConvNeXt-Base, achieving 93.67% accuracy with Grad-CAM visual explainability for diagnostic support.
overview: LUCIAN is a histopathology image classification platform built to assist clinicians in diagnostic workflows by accurately identifying lung cancer subtypes with visual explanation support.
architectureText: Fine-tuned a ConvNeXt-Base backbone using PyTorch. Injected a specialized Grad-CAM (Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping) pipeline to project diagnostic heatmaps onto slide areas, revealing exactly which cellular structures guided the model's classification decisions.
codeBlock:
import torch
import torchvision.models as models

# Fine-Tuned ConvNeXt-Base Backbone
model = models.convnext_base(weights=models.ConvNeXt_Base_Weights.DEFAULT)
model.classifier[2] = torch.nn.Linear(
    in_features=1024, 
    out_features=3, # Carcinoma tissue classes
    bias=True
)
results: Classification Accuracy: Achieved 93.67% overall classification accuracy across complex validation splits.
Explainability Support: Grad-CAM heatmaps highlight precise regions of interest to support clinical peer reviews.
Robust Validation: Validated on multi-class histopathology datasets representing primary lung adenocarcinoma, squamous cell carcinoma, and benign tissues.
